title: prostanthera subalpina
intro: Prostanthera subalpina, also known as the Tasmanian mint bush, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). The leaves of this plant are rich in essential oils and have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Prostanthera subalpina has been traditionally used to relieve digestive issues, soothe respiratory problems, and calm nervous tension, offering numerous health benefits. The plant's leaves can be made into herbal teas, infusions, or dried for use in capsules or tinctures, allowing for various preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
